name: Sentiment Analysis
description: Classifies the affective polarity (positive / negative / neutral, or fine-grained) of user-generated text. Covers pipelines from raw noisy input through preprocessing, inference (Transformer, Lexicon, or LLM), and output normalisation.
conditions: Macro-F1 >= 0.88 (Transformer) or 0.72 (Lexicon) on SST-2 or equivalent benchmark.

evidence.notes: BERT paper — achieves SOTA on SST-2 sentiment benchmark (93.5% accuracy), foundational evidence for LLM-based sentiment analysis. (backfilled — class-to-type migration)
TimeLMs: Diachronic Language Models from Twitter (Barbieri et al., 2022) — 366 citations; Twitter-RoBERTa SOTA baseline for social-media SA.
SemEval-2017 Task 4: Sentiment Analysis in Twitter — 905 citations (ACL Anthology); establishes macro-F1 as primary metric for sentiment analysis.
VADER (Hutto & Gilbert, 2014) — 5755 citations (AAAI/ICWSM); canonical lexicon-based SA baseline; validated on social media corpora.
NLPTown BERT multilingual - 5-class (star-rating) model fine-tuned on product reviews; direct UGC applicability. (backfilled — class-to-type migration)
declare-lab/conv-emotion - Sentiment/emotion recognition in conversations (MELD, IEMOCAP); relevant for threaded comment SA. (backfilled — class-to-type migration)
SST-2 (GLUE benchmark) - Standard binary SA benchmark for cross-model comparison. (backfilled — class-to-type migration)
A Survey on Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis: Tasks, Methods, and Challenges (Zhang et al., 2022) — 419 citations; covers ABSA methods for structured review analysis.
Curated reading list by DeCLaRe Lab; anchored by Poria et al. (2020) IEEE TAC survey on open challenges. (backfilled — class-to-type migration)
